pi_items.source: Confirmed from setup_task.sh and scripts/task_utils.sh which specify Oracle XE connection parameters and use 'hr' schema.
Confirmed from export_result.sh which executes 'SELECT COUNT(*) FROM user_views WHERE view_name = SLA_PERFORMANCE_VW'.
Confirmed from setup_task.sh which cleans it and export_result.sh which validates its existence and length.

Manual calculations validated using SQL INSERTs in setup_task.sh. Checked business hours logic (9am-5pm) against synthetic dates: Ticket 1001 spans Fri Jan 12 to Mon Jan 15 (120 mins). Ticket 1002 spans Fri Jan 5 to Tue Jan 9 with a holiday on Mon Jan 8 (60 mins). Ticket 1003 starts at 20:00 so business minutes begin counting 9:00 next day (15 mins). Ticket 1004 is same day (90 mins).

privileged_info_summary: The SLA data is completely synthetic and seeded by setup scripts. Connection parameters (localhost:1521/XEPDB1, user: hr, password: hr123) are accurate. The requested view name (SLA_PERFORMANCE_VW) and CSV report path (/home/ga/Desktop/sla_report.csv) match the verification scripts. The task relies on 4 artificially planted edge cases (trap_tickets), and their expected business minute lengths (120, 60, 15, and 90 mins) have been verified to accurately map to the inserted dates according to a Monday-Friday 9am-5pm schedule and the public holiday seeded on Jan 8, 2024.
